The Pythagorean theorem states that in a right-angled triangle, the square of the length of the hypotenuse (the side opposite the right angle) is equal to the sum of the squares of the lengths of the other two sides. It is often represented by the formula c² = a² + b², where c, a, and b are the lengths of the sides of the triangle.
